name: Samba de Roda
traditions: Circle dance (roda); hand-clapping rhythms; berimbau and atabaque drums; call-and-response singing; spontaneous celebrations.
historicalBackground: Samba de Roda originated in the Recôncavo region of Bahia with enslaved Africans. It is the ancestor of modern samba and Rio's Carnival music.
description: The ancestral form of samba, a UNESCO-recognized circle dance and music tradition from Bahia's Recôncavo region in Brazil.
